name: Detect a power of two using bitwise tricks

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pow2/pow2.py — a Python program that reads integers from stdin (one per line, may be negative or zero) and for each one writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if it is a positive power of two (1, 2, 4, 8, ...) or 'false' otherwise. n = 0 → false. Negative numbers → false. Use a bitwise approach (the n > 0 and n & (n-1) == 0 trick).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pow2 && python3 pow2.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pow2/pow2.go — a Go program (package main) that reads integers from stdin (one per line, may be negative or zero) and for each one writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if it is a positive power of two or 'false' otherwise. Compile with `go build -o pow2 pow2.go` and run with `./pow2 < input.txt`. Use a bitwise approach (n > 0 && n & (n-1) == 0).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pow2 && go build -o pow2 pow2.go && ./pow2 < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pow2/pow2.c — a C program that reads long integers from stdin (one per line, may be negative or zero) and for each one writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if it is a positive power of two or 'false' otherwise. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pow2 pow2.c` and run with `./pow2 < input.txt`. Use a bitwise approach (n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pow2 && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pow2 pow2.c && ./pow2 < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pow2/pow2.cpp — a C++ program that reads long integers from stdin (one per line, may be negative or zero) and for each one writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if it is a positive power of two or 'false' otherwise. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o pow2 pow2.cpp` and run with `./pow2 < input.txt`. Use a bitwise approach (n > 0 && (n & (n-1)) == 0).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pow2 && g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o pow2 pow2.cpp && ./pow2 < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
